metadata.description: Geospatial threat intelligence for CPS/ICS threat actors and targeted nations

countries.name: Russia
countries.notes: Most prolific state-sponsored ICS/OT threat actor ecosystem globally. Sandworm directly caused two confirmed power outages in Ukraine and developed Industroyer/Crashoverride malware targeting grid SCADA systems. TEMP.Veles deployed TRITON against safety instrumented systems in Saudi Arabia. Berserk Bear (Energetic Bear/Dragonfly) conducts persistent reconnaissance against Western energy infrastructure.

countries.name: China
countries.notes: Volt Typhoon represents the most strategically significant CPS threat from China, pre-positioning in US critical infrastructure (power, water, communications) for potential disruption during geopolitical conflict. RedEcho targeted 10 Indian power sector organizations. Multiple groups focus on long-term persistent access and intellectual property theft from industrial manufacturers.

countries.name: Iran
countries.notes: Iran has demonstrated escalating willingness to conduct destructive attacks against industrial control systems, particularly targeting Gulf state energy infrastructure and Israeli critical systems. Cyber Av3ngers claimed attacks on US water utility Unitronics PLCs. Iranian actors frequently use disk-wiping malware adapted for OT environments. Motivations are geopolitical and retaliatory in nature.

countries.name: North Korea
countries.notes: North Korean actors primarily target ICS environments for intelligence collection and financial gain rather than disruption. Lazarus Group has demonstrated capability to target industrial facilities. Andariel specifically focuses on South Korean defense and energy sectors. Cryptocurrency theft from energy companies funds regime activities. Growing capability in OT reconnaissance tools observed since 2022.

countries.name: Belarus
countries.notes: Belarus operates in close coordination with Russian intelligence services. GhostWriter/UNC1151 primarily conducts influence operations and credential harvesting against NATO-aligned nations, with growing interest in critical infrastructure reconnaissance. Likely provides access and intelligence to Russian threat actors targeting Ukrainian and European OT networks.

countries.name: Pakistan
countries.notes: Pakistani threat actors focus primarily on Indian government and defense targets, with emerging interest in energy and critical infrastructure. Transparent Tribe (APT36) has demonstrated reconnaissance activity against Indian power grid systems. Capability level is moderate; primarily focused on espionage rather than disruption.

countries.name: Vietnam
countries.notes: APT32 (OceanLotus) is a sophisticated group linked to Vietnamese state interests targeting automotive manufacturers and foreign companies operating in Vietnam. Demonstrated capability to compromise manufacturing networks for IP theft. Growing interest in energy sector reconnaissance as Vietnam expands offshore oil production.

countries.name: India
countries.notes: Indian state-linked actors focus primarily on neighboring country government and military targets. Limited documented ICS-specific capability, though reconnaissance of Pakistani and Chinese energy infrastructure has been observed. India is far more frequently a victim of CPS attacks (from China, Pakistan) than a significant originator.

countries.name: Turkey
countries.notes: Turkish-linked actors primarily conduct DNS hijacking and credential theft campaigns targeting regional adversaries. Limited ICS-specific targeting documented; primarily opportunistic access to energy and telecom networks in geopolitically contested areas. Capability development is ongoing.

countries.name: Brazil
countries.notes: Brazilian cybercriminal groups primarily target domestic financial institutions but have impacted industrial networks through ransomware. Brazil's energy sector (Petrobras ecosystem) has experienced multiple ransomware incidents. Primarily financially motivated rather than state-directed; limited OT-specific capability.

countries.name: United States
countries.notes: Most targeted nation globally for CPS/ICS attacks due to critical infrastructure density and geopolitical prominence. Volt Typhoon's pre-positioning in US infrastructure represents a strategic threat for conflict scenarios. Multiple confirmed intrusions into electric utilities, water systems, and oil/gas pipelines. CISA has issued 400+ ICS advisories.

countries.name: Ukraine
countries.notes: Ukraine is the most actively attacked nation in terms of confirmed destructive CPS incidents. Two confirmed power grid blackouts (2015: 230,000 without power; 2016: Kyiv blackout) caused by Sandworm. Industroyer2 deployed against Ukrainian grid in April 2022. Ukraine serves as a live testing ground for Russian ICS offensive cyber capabilities.

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
countries.notes: Saudi Arabia's oil and gas infrastructure is a top target for Iranian and Russian threat actors. The TRITON/TRISIS attack at a Saudi petrochemical facility was the first confirmed attack targeting safety instrumented systems (SIS), capable of causing catastrophic physical damage. Saudi Aramco has faced repeated destructive wiper attacks.

countries.name: Israel
notable_incidents: Israel Water Authority Chlorine Attack Attempt 2020
countries.notes: Israel faces sustained and escalating Iranian-linked attacks against water infrastructure and industrial systems. The 2020 water utility attacks attempted to alter chlorine levels in drinking water supply. Israel has responded with offensive cyber operations against Iranian infrastructure. Post-October 2023 attacks have significantly increased against all critical sectors.

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.notes: UK critical infrastructure regularly targeted by Russian and Chinese state actors. NCSC has issued multiple warnings about Russian actors targeting UK energy, water, and transport OT networks. UK is a primary NATO target for pre-conflict infrastructure pre-positioning. Nuclear facility security has been identified as a concern.

countries.name: Germany
countries.notes: Germany's manufacturing and energy sectors are highly targeted due to industrial density and NATO membership. The 2014 German steel mill attack is one of only a handful of confirmed cases where a cyberattack caused significant physical damage (blast furnace). German automotive and chemical industries are targeted for IP theft and potential disruption.

countries.name: United Arab Emirates
countries.notes: UAE's oil and gas sector and strategic port infrastructure face sustained Iranian-linked attacks. As a major OPEC producer and regional US partner, UAE is a high-value target for Iranian disruption operations. ADNOC and associated infrastructure have been subject to multiple intrusion attempts.

countries.name: Bahrain
countries.notes: Bahrain, host to the US Fifth Fleet, is a consistent target of Iranian cyber operations. The Dustman wiper attack against Bapco (Bahrain Petroleum Company) in 2019 demonstrated Iran's willingness to conduct destructive operations against Gulf state energy infrastructure. Bahrain's small size makes it particularly vulnerable to sustained campaigns.

countries.name: Australia
countries.notes: Australia faces significant Chinese state-sponsored targeting of critical infrastructure. Volt Typhoon activity has been confirmed in Australian networks. The Australian Signals Directorate has issued warnings about pre-positioning in energy and water systems. Mining sector OT networks are targeted for both IP theft and potential disruption of resource exports.

countries.name: Japan
countries.notes: Japan's advanced manufacturing sector and nuclear energy infrastructure face sustained targeting from Chinese and North Korean actors. APT10 (Stone Panda) conducted major industrial espionage campaigns against Japanese manufacturers. Japan's energy transition planning and nuclear restart programs make it a high-value intelligence target. Supply chain attacks against defense manufacturers are escalating.

countries.name: South Korea
notable_incidents: Korea Hydro and Nuclear Power Breach 2014
countries.notes: South Korea faces the highest volume of North Korean ICS-focused attacks globally. The Korea Hydro and Nuclear Power breach resulted in leaked nuclear facility manuals and reactor blueprints. Andariel specifically targets South Korean defense and energy infrastructure. China-linked actors also target semiconductor and electronics manufacturing for IP theft.

countries.name: Taiwan
countries.notes: Taiwan faces the most intensive and strategically significant Chinese cyber threat of any nation. Chinese actors are pre-positioning in Taiwan's power grid, semiconductor manufacturing, and telecom networks for potential conflict scenarios. Disrupting Taiwan's electricity supply or semiconductor production would have global economic consequences. Threat activity has sharply escalated since 2022.

countries.name: Canada
countries.notes: Canada's oil sands, natural gas pipelines, and hydroelectric infrastructure face threats from both Russian and Chinese actors. As a US neighbor sharing interconnected energy grids, attacks on Canadian infrastructure can cascade to US systems. CSE (Communications Security Establishment) has confirmed active intrusions in energy sector OT networks.

countries.name: France
countries.notes: France operates the largest nuclear fleet in Europe (56 reactors providing ~70% of electricity), making it a high-value target. Russian actors have targeted French energy and government systems. Chinese APT10 conducted sustained espionage against French aerospace and nuclear technology companies. ANSSI has issued multiple ICS threat advisories.

countries.name: Netherlands
countries.notes: The Netherlands hosts the Port of Rotterdam (Europe's largest) and major chemical and energy infrastructure. The MIVD has publicly attributed multiple infrastructure intrusion attempts to Russian GRU. Shell and other energy companies headquartered in the Netherlands face persistent targeting. Dutch semiconductor equipment manufacturers (ASML) are targeted for technology theft.
